channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Why IAF Is Staying Away from TEDBF: ORCA Debate, Rafale Push and AMCA Priorities Explained
item.description: The Indian Air Force’s distance from the Navy-led Twin Engine Deck Based Fighter (TEDBF) program is not an institutional disconnect but a consequence of divergent operational requirements, lifecycle economics, and a tightly packed modernization roadmap. While a land-based derivative—often discussed as ORCA (Omni-Role Combat Aircraft)—has periodically surfaced, the IAF’s current priorities make such a pivot unlikely in the near term.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Scorpène Evolved: India May Get World’s First LiB-Powered Submarine With BrahMos-NG VLS
item.description: Naval Group has significantly upgraded its offer to the Indian Navy by proposing the Scorpène® Evolved, a next-generation variant that marks a substantial technological leap over the existing Kalvari-class submarines. The proposal is designed to bridge the capability gap between the completed Project-75 program and the upcoming Project-75I, while leveraging India’s existing submarine production ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: After DAC Nod, DRDO Issues Key Tenders for Ghatak Stealth UCAV Prototype
item.description: India’s ambitious Ghatak Unmanned Combat Aerial Vehicle (UCAV) program has entered a decisive development phase following the Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) clearing the procurement of four squadrons (approximately 60–64 units) in late March 2026. With this approval, the Aeronautical Development Establishment (ADE) has accelerated the transition from the SWiFT (Stealth Wing Flying Testbed) demonstrator to the full-scale Remotely Piloted Strike Aircraft (RPSA) configuration.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Engine Journey: F414 Deal Is More Than a ‘Half-Step’: Why Critics Are Missing the Bigger Picture
item.description: Criticism of the HAL–GE F414 engine co-production deal as merely a “half-step” toward propulsion autonomy reflects a narrow interpretation of what technological progress in aerospace actually looks like. While it is correct that full intellectual property transfer—especially in core hot-section technologies—remains unlikely, framing the deal as a strategic shortfall overlooks the practical and industrial gains that are far more consequential in the near to medium term.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Tata Advanced Systems Develops Next-Gen Unmanned Ground Vehicle for Defence Use
item.description: Tata Advanced Systems Limited (TASL) has unveiled a cutting-edge Unmanned Ground Vehicle (UGV) that represents a major stride in India’s push for indigenous unmanned systems tailored to modern military needs. This fully tracked platform, designed and developed in-house, combines rugged durability with versatile operational capabilities, positioning it as a valuable asset for defence forces operating in challenging terrains.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Brahmastra Explosives Offers 122mm GRAD Rockets with 40 km Range for Immediate Supply
item.description: Brahmastra Explosives & Ammunition has announced the immediate availability of 122mm GRAD rockets featuring an extended range of up to 40 kilometers, signaling a significant offering in the artillery munitions segment at a time when demand for rocket artillery systems continues to rise globally. The announcement highlights the company’s readiness to support both immediate operational requirements and sustained supply commitments through secured manufacturing arrangements with its original equipment manufacturer (OEM) partners.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: CSIR-NAL Develops Indigenous Nickel–Titanium Shape Memory Alloys, Boosting India’s Smart Materials Capability
item.description: CSIR–National Aerospace Laboratories (NAL) has achieved a significant milestone in advanced materials science with the indigenous development of Nickel–Titanium Shape Memory Alloys (NiTi SMAs), a class of smart materials known for their unique ability to “remember” shapes and exhibit superelastic behavior. This breakthrough places India among a select group of nations capable of designing and producing high-performance SMAs for both aerospace and biomedical applications.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Top Zelensky Aide Meets, Ajit Doval, S Jaishankar To Discuss Path To Peace
item.description: Ukraine’s National Security and Defence Council Secretary Rustem Umerov on Friday held separate, high-stakes meetings with External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar and National Security Advisor Ajit Doval on the prospects of “achieving lasting peace” in the conflict-hit nation.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Navy Veteran Can’t Return From Qatar Despite Commuted Death Sentence
item.description: A former Indian Navy official, whose death sentence was commuted by a Qatari court in 2023 along with seven others, has been unable to return to India due to detention in a separate case, the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pune Airport Runway Shut After Air Force Aircraft’s ‘Hard Landing’
item.description: The Indian Air Force (IAF) late Friday night said the runway at Pune airport is temporarily unavailable following an incident involving one of its aircraft. It said efforts are underway to operationalise the runway. “Pune runway is temporarily unavailable due to an incident involving an IAF aircraft. The aircrew are safe and there is no damage to civil property,” the IAF said on social media.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Welcomes Israel–Lebanon Ceasefire, Every Step Leading Towards Peace
item.description: India on Friday welcomed the ceasefire announced between Israel and Lebanon, expressing support for every step that leads towards peace. While addressing a weekly media briefing in New Delhi on Friday, Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said, “We welcome the ceasefire. We welcome every step that leads towards peace.”
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Delimitation Exercise: India Rejects Pak Attempts To Intrude Into Internal Matters
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) on Friday firmly dismissed Pakistan’s statement about the delimitation process in India. Islamabad had reacted to reports that India’s Delimitation Bill 2026 outlines a legal framework for conducting constituency delimitation in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Over 2300 Indians Safely Brought Back From Iran Since West Asia Conflict Began: MEA
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) said on Friday that India has safely brought back home 2,361 people since the beginning of the conflict in West Asia. Addressing a weekly media briefing in New Delhi on Friday, MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal stated that these 2,361 people include Indian students, fishermen and also three foreigners.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Four Maoists, Including Commander Carrying Rs 15 Lakh Reward, Killed In Jharkhand Encounter
item.description: Four dreaded Maoists, including commander Sahdev Mahto, who carried a Rs 15 lakh reward on his head, were killed by security forces in an encounter on the Chatra-Hazaribagh district border on Friday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Chhattisgarh: Maoist Explosives Cache Recovered In Bijapur District
item.description: In a major anti-Maoist operation, security forces recovered a large quantity of explosive materials concealed by Maoists in the dense forests of Annapur under the jurisdiction of Annapur Police Station at Farsegad in Bijapur district.?
item.dc:creator: admin
